name: Bevy
description: Flagship pure-Rust engine; ECS-first, data-driven, wgpu renderer, huge community and plugin ecosystem

kind.comment: ECS-first full engine with a data-driven scene system and asset pipeline; note it does not yet ship an official visual editor.

license.comment: Dual-licensed: "Open Source under the permissive MIT or Apache 2.0 licenses."

documentation.comment: Official Learn section (Quick Start guide + examples); API docs at docs.rs/bevy.

first-release.comment: Bevy 0.1 was first announced/published in August 2020.

maintenance-status.comment: Repository last pushed 2026-07-06; roughly quarterly releases with a large contributor base.

maturity.comment: Still pre-1.0 (0.19) with breaking changes and a migration guide every release, but production titles have shipped; anchored to API churn per the guide rather than popularity.

backing.comment: Developed as a community open-source project stewarded by the nonprofit Bevy Foundation; funded by donations and sponsorships.

implementation.comment: Everything is implemented in Rust with only platform glue; no bundled non-Rust engine.

architecture.comment: ECS is the dominant and primary data model; there is no scene-graph node tree.

built-in-ecs.comment: bevy_ecs is the primary way games are structured; a headline feature.

dimensionality.comment: First-party 2D (sprites, texture atlases) and 3D (PBR, glTF, lighting) renderers.

scripting.comment: Core gameplay logic is written in Rust; Lua/Rhai/WASM scripting exists only via community plugins, not first-party.

data-driven-scenes.comment: RON-serialized scenes plus the new Bevy Scene Notation (BSN, bsn! macro) introduced in 0.19 for authoring scenes as data.

reflection-serialization.comment: First-class runtime reflection via bevy_reflect, underpinning the scene (de)serialization system.

hot-reload.comment: Built-in hot asset reloading and scene hot reloading; experimental code hot-patching also exists.

visual-editor.comment: No official graphical scene/level editor ships yet; long planned but not released.

live-inspector.comment: No first-party inspector; the de-facto community tool bevy-inspector-egui provides a runtime entity/component/world inspector.

asset-pipeline.comment: AssetServer offers async loading of many formats plus an asset processing pipeline and hot reloading.

cli-project-tooling.comment: Official bevy_cli (bevy new) plus maintained starter templates (e.g. bevy_new_2d).

debug-profiling-tools.comment: Built-in diagnostics plugins (FrameTimeDiagnostics) and Tracy integration; egui-based overlays available via community plugins.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling (rust-analyzer works out of the box); no dedicated IDE plugin.

rendering-backend.comment: Renders through wgpu, which targets Vulkan, Metal, DirectX 12, WebGL2 and WebGPU.

lighting.comment: Clustered forward is the default; a deferred path and lightmap-based baked GI are available; experimental raytraced GI (Solari) is in progress in 0.19.

shadows.comment: Real-time shadow mapping; 0.19 added contact shadows.

custom-shaders.comment: Custom WGSL shaders and render pipelines via the composable Render Graph.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Sprite batching and texture atlases are built-in; tilemaps are provided by de-facto community plugins (e.g. bevy_ecs_tilemap).

particle-system.comment: No built-in particle/VFX system; provided by community plugins such as bevy_hanabi and bevy_enoki.

post-processing.comment: Bloom, tonemapping, TAA/FXAA and, as of 0.19, vignette and lens distortion effects.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in font/text rendering with layout, variable fonts and font-family selection.

physics.comment: No physics in core; de-facto choices are the Avian plugin and bevy_rapier.

audio.comment: bevy_audio ships in core (rodio-based); Kira is a popular alternative via the bevy_kira_audio plugin.

input.comment: First-party keyboard/mouse, gamepad and touch input.

animation.comment: Skeletal rigging with blending and morph targets via the animation graph; sprite-frame and tween animation come from community plugins.

ui-system.comment: Built-in bevy_ui (flexbox layout) plus the growing Feathers widget set; egui is available via a plugin.

networking.comment: No networking in core; de-facto plugins are Lightyear, renet and bevy_replicon.

windows.comment: Listed among "all major platforms: Windows, MacOS, Linux, Web, iOS, Android".

macos.comment: Officially supported (Apple Silicon + Intel via Metal/wgpu).

linux.comment: Officially supported on both X11 and Wayland.

web-wasm.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly and runs in the browser (WebGL2/WebGPU).

ios.comment: Officially listed as a supported platform; a mobile example is maintained in-repo.

android.comment: Officially listed as a supported platform; a mobile example is maintained in-repo.

consoles.comment: No official console support; console ports exist only as unofficial/NDA-gated community forks (e.g. Switch), with no publicly reproducible first-party path.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured on a standard machine this pass. A cold release build of a minimal Bevy app is a well-known multi-minute cost; the guide requires a measured number, so left null.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured. Highly config-dependent: fast (~2-10s) with Bevy's dynamic-linking feature plus a fast linker (mold/lld), noticeably slower with the default static build.

binary-size.comment: Not measured this pass. Minimal Bevy native binaries are known to be large, but no reliable current stripped-release measurement was available.

web-bundle-size.comment: Not measured this pass. A minimal Bevy WASM bundle is multi-MB and grows quickly with features; no reliable current post-wasm-opt figure was available.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured; the guide says to prefer null over a guess for idle CPU/memory.

github-stars.comment: Snapshot from the GitHub API on 2026-07-06.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level bevy crate as of 2026-07-06.

shipped-titles.comment: Tiny Glade is the widely-documented commercial title built with Bevy; numerous indie and jam games also use it.

community-size.comment: Very active Discord and forum plus hundreds of GitHub contributors.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: The Bevy Assets registry catalogs hundreds of third-party plugins and crates covering physics, audio, UI, networking, tilemaps and more.

learning-resources.comment: Official Quick Start guide, a large examples directory, and an active tutorial/video community; the guide names Bevy as the exemplar for a 5.
